title: Exploiting cross-site scripting vulnerabilities
attack_vector:
Web Security Academy
Cross-site scripting
Exploiting
Exploiting cross-site scripting vulnerabilities
The traditional way to prove that you've found a cross-site scripting vulnerability is to create a popup using the
alert()
function. This isn't because XSS has anything to do with popups; it's simply a way to prove that you can execute arbitrary JavaScript on a given domain. You might notice some people using
alert(document.domain)
. This is a way of making it explicit which domain the JavaScript
